nativeRange.habitat: Mixed coral and rubble areas of lagoon and seaward reefs, from the lower surge zone down to roughly 40-45 m.
nativeRange.biotope: Tropical coral reef with abundant live rock, branching corals, and cover, typically encountered as solitary individuals.

diet.notes: In the wild, has been documented feeding on filamentous algae and small invertebrates. In captivity, generally accepts a varied diet of marine flakes and pellets, frozen mysis and brine shrimp, chopped meaty seafoods, and dried marine algae or seaweed sheets.

avoidWith: newly added tankmates after the damsel is established

summary: Small Indo-Pacific damselfish with a blue body fading to yellow/orange and a black ocellus on the dorsal fin. Generally peaceful as a juvenile and tends to become territorial with age, but stays reef-safe and is one of the hardier marine fish for beginners.
careNotes: Has been reported as easy to keep and reef-safe; generally peaceful as a juvenile but tends to become semi-aggressive or territorial toward other damselfish and similarly sized fish as it matures. Best kept singly in modest reef setups; aggression is more manageable in larger tanks with plenty of live rock and broken sightlines. Adults are commonly reported around 7.5-10 cm (FishBase max ~10 cm FL; LiveAquaria/AquariumDomain ~3-3.5 in). LiveAquaria and AquariumDomain quote a 30 US gallon minimum (~114 L), while general aquarium-keeper guidance suggests 55+ US gal (~208 L) is more comfortable for adult specimens, especially alongside peaceful tankmates — a 150 L tank is a reasonable middle ground that respects the species' adult territoriality. Provide abundant live rock with caves and branching coral for shelter, plus open mid-water space; moderate to strong flow is appreciated. Generally accepts prepared foods readily, including marine flakes, pellets, frozen mysis/brine shrimp and dried marine algae. Wild populations comprise several color forms (geographic variants), so coloration can vary somewhat between specimens.
breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing during breeding. Eggs are demersal and adhere to the substrate; males have been reported to guard and aerate the eggs. Captive breeding is not commonly documented for this species.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Care level Easy, temperament Aggressive (matures into semi-aggressive/territorial), reef-safe, water parameters (pH 8.1-8.4, dKH 8-12, SG 1.020-1.025, 72-78 F), 30 US gal minimum, max 3.5 in, omnivore, native range stated as Fiji. Browsed via product page reached from /collections/damselfish.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed taxonomy (Pomacentridae), max length 10.0 cm FL, depth 0-45 m, Pacific Ocean distribution (Moluccas to Samoa, Izu Islands to Rowley Shoals and New Caledonia), water temperature 25.4-28.9 C, omnivorous (algae + small invertebrates), oviparous with male egg-guarding, IUCN Least Concern.

additional.name: AquariumDomain (Princess Damselfish profile)
additional.notes: Cross-checked size (~3 in), temperament (peaceful as juvenile, semi-aggressive with age), reef-safe, 30 gal minimum but better with more room, water parameters (72-80 F, pH 8.1-8.4, dKH 8-12, SG 1.020-1.025), prefers moderate to strong flow with abundant live rock.

fish.breedingNotes: Pairs form during breeding and spawn demersal eggs that adhere to the substrate; males have been reported to guard and aerate the clutch. Larval rearing is not commonly documented for hobbyists.
